bead.title: Story 16.3: Specs + cross-cutting e2e + raw-transcript-viewed audit even: part 2
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-70a432b5 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and FEAT-019 specs are missing the documentation sections required to make the Story-16 run-detail page and its evidence browser official. Without spec updates, the run-detail page, trust boundary statement, and raw-transcript audit event are undocumented platform behaviors.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-*.md: missing a Run-detail-page subsection describing the trust boundary ('Anyone with project read access sees raw prompts, responses, stderr, tool args') and evidence browser whitelist (allowed extensions + 64KB cap).
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-*.md: Run resolver contract does not include prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement, or normalized ToolCallEntry.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-*.md: review surface does not point to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- No 'raw transcript viewed' audit event exists in the server or schema.
- Depends on ddx-f04faa86 (16.1 page) and ddx-4cd64068 (16.2 evidence tab).

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-008: add Run-detail-page subsection with trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist (*.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB; no Markdown rendering in evidence tab).
- Update FEAT-010: extend Run resolver contract with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement; document normalized ToolCallEntry as canonical store populated at drain.
- Update FEAT-019: review surface section updated to link to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- Add 'raw transcript viewed' audit event in server (emitted on prompt/response/transcript access).
- Add cross-cutting e2e: full prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence-list end-to-end via Playwright.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend implementation (that is Story 16.1 and 16.2).
- New auth or role-based access control beyond what the trust boundary statement documents.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- FEAT-019 spec updated with link from review surface to /runs/[runId].
- 'raw transcript viewed' audit event emitted on access and covered by test (TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed).
- Cross-cutting e2e test exercises full prompt visible + response visible + tool trace visible + evidence list visible: test('full stack: prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence', ...) Playwright test.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
bead.acceptance:
1. FEAT-019 spec updated with link from review surface to /runs/[runId].
2. 'raw transcript viewed' audit event emitted on access and covered by test (TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed).
3. Cross-cutting e2e test exercises full prompt visible + response visible + tool trace visible + evidence list visible: test('full stack: prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence', ...) Playwright test.
4. cd cli && go test ./... passes.

events.body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"ambiguous_scope: The bead is not safely refinable as written: it mixes a narrow child slice with stale parent scope (FEAT-008/010/019 doc work, audit-event implementation, and e2e coverage), and the provided anchors stop at file globs with no file:line evidence. Rewriting would require either inventing that evidence or choosing which of the broader requirements stay in scope, so operator input is needed before claim.","fingerprint":"4a4c61c4c495026c402b5fc3d74cf2535cc4223730d9e55d287891592f499c0c","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment"}

{"rationale":"The bead has numbered, testable acceptance criteria and names concrete verification artifacts, but it misses several execution-ready requirements: the title is not a clear imperative subsystem-scoped summary, the description lacks file:line-rooted current-state evidence, the required `lefthook run pre-commit` and package-scoped `go test` gate are absent, labels do not include `area` and `kind`, and deps are not explicit in the bead metadata.","score":2,"suggested_fixes":["Rewrite the title as an imperative subsystem-scoped change, for example `docs: add FEAT-019 deep-link to /runs/[runId] and raw-transcript audit coverage`.","Add file:line citations in ROOT CAUSE for the FEAT-019 doc section and the server audit-event gap, and collapse the duplicate PROPOSED FIX / NON-SCOPE blocks into one clean prompt.","Add an explicit `deps` field listing `ddx-f04faa86` and `ddx-4cd64068` with one-line why each dependency matters.","Extend acceptance with a package-scoped command such as `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/...` and add `lefthook run pre-commit`.","Add missing labels for `area:*` and `kind:*`, keeping `phase:*` and the cross-reference labels."],"waivers_applied":[]}
